

**Nexus E-Commerce Platform**

A full-stack multi-role e-commerce system with chat, fraud detection, and analytics

**Group 23** 

Muhammad Ali Amir

Muhammad Ali

**1\. Project Overview**

Nexus is a full stack e commerce platform designed to simulate a real world online marketplace. The system targets the retail domain where multiple types of users interact within the same application.

The main goal of this project is to solve the problem of managing a multi role system where customers, vendors, couriers, and admins all access the same data but with different permissions and responsibilities.

Customers can browse products, manage carts, place orders, and track deliveries. Vendors manage products and view their sales. Couriers handle delivery operations. Admins monitor the entire system including fraud detection and analytics.

This project focuses on applying database concepts such as transactions, constraints, and indexing in a real system.

---

**2\. Tech Stack**

The following technologies were used in building the system.

Backend

| Technology | Purpose |
| ----- | ----- |
| Node.js | Server runtime |
| Express.js | API routing |
| PostgreSQL | Relational database |
| pg pool | Connection pooling |
| JWT | Authentication |
| bcrypt | Password hashing |
| Socket.IO | Real time communication |
| dotenv | Environment variables |
| swagger ui express | API documentation |

Frontend

| Technology | Purpose |
| ----- | ----- |
| React with Vite | User interface |
| Tailwind CSS | Styling |
| Axios | API requests |
| React Router | Navigation |
| Socket.IO Client | Chat |
| Lucide React | Icons |

---

**3\. System Architecture**

The system follows a client server architecture with three main layers.

The frontend is built using React and handles all user interactions. It communicates with the backend using HTTP requests and WebSockets for real time chat.

The backend is built using Express and handles authentication, authorization, business logic, and API endpoints. It also manages transactions and communicates with the database.

The database is PostgreSQL which stores all system data including users, products, orders, and messages.

Flow of request

1. Request sent from frontend with token  
2. Backend verifies authentication  
3. Role is checked  
4. Controller processes request  
5. Database query is executed  
6. Response is returned

---

**4\. UI Examples**

**Customer Cart and Checkout**

This page allows users to manage cart items, apply coupons, and place orders. It is important because it handles the main transaction of the system. The checkout ensures stock validation and rollback if any issue occurs.  
Applied a valid coupon and amount reduced.

![][image1]

**Admin Fraud Dashboard**

This page shows all flagged orders. Admin can view details and take actions like banning users. It is required to monitor suspicious activity and maintain system security.

**![][image2]**

**Real Time Chat**

This interface allows communication between users. Customers can talk to vendors and couriers. It is required to keep communication inside the platform instead of using external tools.  
**Customer’s Side**![][image3]

**Vendor’s Side![][image4]**

---

**5\. Setup and Installation**

Prerequisites

| Requirement | Version |
| ----- | ----- |
| Node.js | 18 or higher |
| npm | 9 or higher |
| PostgreSQL | 14 or higher |

**Installation Steps**

**Backend**

cd backend  
 npm install

**Frontend**

cd frontend  
 npm install

| Variable | Description | Example |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| PORT | Server port | 3000 |
| DB\_HOST | Database host | localhost |
| DB\_PORT | Database port | 5432 |
| DB\_NAME | Database name | ecommerce\_db |
| DB\_USER | Database user | postgres |
| DB\_PASSWORD | Database password | password |
| JWT\_SECRET | Secret key | random\_string |
| JWT\_EXPIRES\_IN | Token expiry | 7d |

**Database Initialization**

1. Create database ecommerce\_db  
2. Run schema.sql  
3. Run migrations or seed.sql if provided

**Running the Project**

**Backend**

cd backend  
 node server.js

**Frontend**

cd frontend  
 npm run dev

---

**6\. User Roles**

| Role | Can Do | Cannot Do |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Admin | Manage users, products, fraud | Place orders |
| Customer | Browse, order, review | Access admin features |
| Vendor | Manage products | Access other vendors data |
| Courier | Handle deliveries | Manage products |

| Role | Email | Password |

---

**7\. Feature Walkthrough**

| Feature | Role | Endpoint | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Product browsing | All | GET products | View products |
| Cart management | Customer | Cart APIs | Add or remove items |
| Order placement | Customer | POST orders | Place order with transaction |
| Reviews | Customer | POST reviews | Add product review |
| Vendor products | Vendor | Vendor APIs | Manage products |
| Courier orders | Courier | Courier APIs | Accept and deliver orders |
| Fraud monitoring | Admin | Admin fraud API | View flagged orders |
| Chat | All | WebSocket | Real time messaging |

---

**8\. Transaction Scenarios**

Order Placement

Trigger  
 User clicks place order

Operations

* Fetch cart  
* Validate stock  
* Apply coupon  
* Insert order  
* Insert order items  
* Reduce stock  
* Clear cart  
* Run fraud detection

Rollback occurs if

* Cart is empty  
* Stock is insufficient  
* Coupon invalid  
* Any database error

Fraud User Ban

Trigger  
 Admin bans user

Operations

* Block user  
* Cancel orders  
* Restore stock  
* Close fraud flags

Rollback if any step fails

---

**9\. ACID Compliance**

| Property | Implementation |
| ----- | ----- |
| Atomicity | Transactions using begin and rollback |
| Consistency | Constraints on tables |
| Isolation | Database default isolation |
| Durability | Write ahead logging |

---

**10\. Indexing and Performance**

| Index | Reason |
| ----- | ----- |
| Users email | Faster login |
| Orders user\_id | Order history |
| Product variants | Product loading |
| Messages | Chat queries |

Indexes improve query speed and reduce load on database.

---

**11\. API Reference**

| Method | Route | Auth | Purpose |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| POST | auth register | No | Create user |
| POST | auth login | No | Login |
| GET | products | No | List products |
| POST | orders | Yes | Place order |
| GET | orders | Yes | View orders |
| POST | reviews | Yes | Add review |
| GET | admin fraud | Yes | View fraud |

Full details available in swagger documentation.

---

**12\. Known Issues and Limitations**

| Issue | Explanation |
| ----- | ----- |
| No email system | Only in app notifications |
| Images stored as text base64 | Not efficient |
| Fraud rules fixed | Cannot change dynamically |
|  |  |
|  |  |
